text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy little village, there lived a fox named Fredrick. Fredrick loved exploring outdoors. One sunny afternoon he decided to visit the Whispering Woods. The trees leaned mysteriously towards him.

Inside one oak leaf he pulled. A tiny owl perched politely.

"I know lot about birds," Fredrick said.

The owl croaked warmly.

One owl feather drifted away.

Thoughtful owl.

"I like that."

Fly south.

Why so solemn?

You lost something.

Just a feather.

That's cruel.

I always lose things.

Do you think happily?

I think.

Fly somewhere nice.

So do I.

Here.



You're silly.

I just like cats.

You like them?

You're cool.

Come here often.



You’re wise.

You know my name?

I prefer numbers.

Numbers great.

You think?

You got a good eye.

Maybe I do.

You look nice.

I think.































Good night.




























Did you see the
